summary: The District of Columbia Courts, on behalf of the Office of the General Counsel (OGC), seeks a qualified vendor to provide a cloud-hosted, configurable commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS) Legal Case and Document Management System to modernize OGC's legal operations and achieve business and legal process efficiencies. The system must deliver comprehensive functionality including document and case management, litigation hold tracking, contract management, eDiscovery capabilities, generative AI features for automated workflows and intelligent scanning, and robust search across approximately 1.2 TB of confidential and privileged materials comprising roughly 390,000 files across 10,000 folders. Technical requirements include compatibility with Citrix VDI, Microsoft Entra ID single sign-on, NIST 800-53 r5/800-37 r2 security controls, FedRAMP authorized cloud hosting, Section 508 compliance, and integration with the Courts' existing Microsoft 365, Azure, and Oracle-based enterprise IT environment. The contract is structured as a firm-fixed price award with a base year and four one-year option periods, requiring delivery of software licenses within 14 calendar days of award, followed by installation, document migration, implementation, five consecutive days of on-site post-implementation support and end-user training, and ongoing maintenance and support including a phone-based help desk Monday through Saturday from 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM Eastern Time. OGC currently has six staff members requiring licenses with potential expansion to up to ten licenses in future option years, and the procurement is issued on an unrestricted open market basis with award to the responsible offeror whose proposal is most advantageous considering price and other evaluation factors. This initiative is strategically significant as OGC currently has no legacy legal or document management system, and the new system will support critical functions including legislative drafting, contract review, employment advisory services, eDiscovery management, and representation before administrative tribunals.
office: Administrative Services Division, Procurement and Contracts Branch
title: Legal Case & Document Management System
